description_long: This object is a heavily damaged and partially destroyed airplane fuselage, with jagged, torn metal edges and large holes exposing the interior. The exterior is primarily white with patches of blue and gray, and there are remnants of structural elements such as a vertical stabilizer and possible wing root. The surface is rough and weathered, with visible signs of corrosion, dirt, and debris. Some internal framework and wiring are exposed, and the overall structure appears hollow and lightweight due to the extensive damage. The object is missing most of its wings, engines, and landing gear, leaving only a fragmented shell. The remaining structure is irregular and asymmetrical, with broken panels and bent supports.
description: A severely damaged, hollow airplane fuselage with jagged metal edges, exposed framework, and weathered white, blue, and gray surfaces.
description_view_0: This view highlights the vertical stabilizer with blue paint, jagged torn edges, and a partially open fuselage with visible internal supports.
description_view_1: The side view shows the hollow interior, broken panels, and the extent of the damage along the fuselage, with more exposed framework.
description_view_2: This angle emphasizes the open, fragmented structure, with the interior and underside of the fuselage visible, showing debris and missing components.
description_view_3: From above, the blue-topped stabilizer and the rough, uneven surface of the fuselage are prominent, with the hollow interior and broken sections clearly visible.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with straight, broken wing roots and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: No engines visible; engine mounts or nacelles are missing or destroyed.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer; horizontal stabilizers are missing or destroyed.
attribute_landing_gear_type: No landing gear visible; gear bays or mounts may be present but are damaged or absent.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit; canopy and windows are missing or destroyed, with the cockpit area exposed.
